description: Development tooling pinned to old versions — CI uses these, so vulns matter
review_checklist: Map dev tools to known CVEs
Check for compatibility with modern Python versions
Identify dev tools that execute downloaded code

ground_truth.description: pytest 5.4.3 is from 2020. pytest 8.x is current. While dev-only, pytest plugins can have remote-code-execution implications during test discovery — not as critical as runtime deps but still worth updating.
black==19.10b0 is a beta release. Stable black has been available since 21.0+. Beta in a dev requirements file is a code smell but rarely a security issue.
flake8 3.7.9 (2019) — current is 7.x. Mostly cosmetic but pyflakes/pycodestyle rules have evolved.
tox 3.14.6 has CVE-2018-19919 (path traversal). Patched long ago; upgrade to 4.x.
Sphinx 2.4.4 has multiple historical CVEs and supports old Python only. Sphinx 7.x is current. Documentation builders that fetch external resources can be a supply-chain entry point.
